_meta.review_warning: This is an AI-drafted v0 template. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner (or Chris's insurer's risk team) and approved. The drafter (Claude) is not a WHS consultant.

hazards.title: Manual handling — temporary fencing panels
hazards.risk: Lifting/carrying 2.4m fence panels and feet; awkward shape, ~25kg per panel

controls.title: Two-person lift for all fence panels and feet

hazards.title: Public vehicle / pedestrian conflict
hazards.risk: Set-up disturbs public footpath / nature strip / driveway crossover; vehicle or pedestrian struck-by

controls.title: Witches hats + barrier tape on public edge before any disturbance
Council permit for any footpath occupation; comply with conditions
Hi-vis long sleeve at all times during set-up

hazards.title: Existing site hazards (uneven ground, glass, used needles, debris)
hazards.risk: Walking / falling / cuts / sharps exposure during initial walk and clean

controls.title: Site supervisor + crew walk site before any other activity; flag and remove hazards

hazards.title: Existing pool on site — drowning
hazards.risk: Worker fall into existing pool; child / neighbour access during construction window

controls.title: Existing pool barrier remains in place and inspected; any breach permitted only with temporary equivalent barrier
Pool surrounds marked no-go for non-essential workers
Solid temporary cover or hard-stand if barrier must come down for access

ppe.title: SPF50+ sunscreen + wide-brim or legionnaire hat

tasks: Pre-start: supervisor walks site with owner / their rep; confirm scope, services, sensitive plantings/structures.
Confirm DBYD search current within 30 days; carry plan on site.
If footpath / nature strip occupation required, confirm Council permit on site and complies with conditions.
Remove existing site hazards: glass, sharps, debris; bag and bin per council disposal rules.
Confirm existing pool barrier (if any) is intact and compliant; do not breach without temporary equivalent.
Mark out site boundary, equipment lay-down area, spoil stockpile area, materials area, vehicle parking, pedestrian path.
Erect temporary perimeter fencing (1.8m chain-mesh) on all street-facing and shared-neighbour boundaries; cable-tie all panels; weighted/staked feet.
Install signage at the entry gate: site safety QR code (induction), 'Construction Site — Authorised Entry Only', 'PPE Required', site supervisor + emergency contact phone numbers.
Position first aid kit and fire extinguisher at gate; note location on the SSSP.
Confirm welfare: toilet provision (portable or with owner permission to use dwelling), shade, drinking water.
Position waste bin / skip in agreed location.
Photograph completed set-up; upload to project Hub for owner sign-off.
